scenario_results.name: Provider VM platform fit decision matrix
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves provider-specific VM sizing and platform-fit recommendations with workload facts, quota assumptions, disk constraints, failure-domain caveats, source basis, and confidence state.

scenario_results.name: Production availability and placement architecture
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves availability-zone, placement, backup, replication, failover, and validation guidance while keeping application data-layer ownership out of scope.

scenario_results.name: Hybrid hypervisor to cloud migration cutover
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves staged migration, discovery, replication, pilot, cutover, validation, rollback, failback, and owner-handoff behavior.

scenario_results.name: Terraform VM implementation mapping
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves provider resource mapping, Terraform state and drift evidence, validation gates, rollback boundaries, and refusal to execute live operations.

scenario_results.name: VM boot and disk attach incident
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves ordered incident triage across image, disk, host, quota, attachment, encryption, and guest OS evidence with missing-evidence escalation.

scenario_results.name: Post-resize or host-maintenance performance regression
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves metric-driven diagnostics for resize, family capability, disk throttling, host maintenance, noisy-neighbor, quota, and guest OS causes without fabricated root cause.

scenario_results.name: Backup and disaster recovery design review
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves RTO/RPO mapping, restore-test evidence, replication constraints, DR drill planning, failover/failback handling, and approval-required risk decisions.

scenario_results.name: Cost reliability and isolation tradeoff
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves segmentation of reserved, interruptible, and dedicated-host options by criticality, interruption tolerance, isolation, quota, commitment risk, and approval boundary.

scenario_results.name: Tenant-specific isolation adaptation
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves tenant-specific adaptation that preserves public provider baseline, host isolation records, data classification, backup segregation, observability evidence, and authority constraints.

scenario_results.name: Pre-production VM platform validation
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves validation checklist coverage for source freshness, sizing, quota, availability, backup restore test, observability, IaC drift, change approval, rollback, and no regressions.

scenario_results.name: Application architecture and code request refusal
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves clear refusal of application architecture, data model, and code-level optimization while preserving VM evidence for handoff.

scenario_results.name: IAM and endpoint security adjacent-lane routing
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves IAM, endpoint security, OS hardening policy, and security governance routing with only virtualization implications retained.

scenario_results.name: Network-only architecture reroute
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of network-only architecture and routing to a network or cloud landing-zone specialist.

scenario_results.name: Tenant credential and live provisioning refusal
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of credential handling, production provisioning, live resize, and migration execution with safe handoff to authorized tenant operators.

scenario_results.name: Unsupported nested virtualization and firmware tuning
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves low-confidence escalation when nested virtualization, custom firmware, accelerator passthrough, or managed cloud behavior is unsupported or unverifiable.

scenario_results.name: Vendor contract or policy interpretation escalation
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of legal, contractual, procurement, or provider commitment interpretation while producing a technical evidence package for authorized owners.

scenario_results.name: Missing-facts generic virtualization request
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves the lane will not answer as a renamed generic cloud helper and stops until workload, provider, quota, tenancy, backup, telemetry, and authority facts are supplied.

scenario_results.name: Conflicting public source and tenant policy
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves source-policy conflict handling, source traceability, no unsupported synthesis, and escalation for precedence, cost, risk, and policy exception decisions.

notes: All 18 scenarios passed the standard 0.90 threshold while preserving the legacy virtualization scope and expanding source-backed workflow, evidence, and functionality coverage.
Functionality coverage includes six shared abilities, two general workflow abilities, and the cross-industry artifact-awareness ability.
Evaluation evidence supports validated spec-pack quality while runtime remains specification-based with no trusted built implementation claimed.
